target.name: Black Forest Labs
target.founder: Robin Rombach, Andreas Blattmann, Patrick Esser

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $3.2B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $5.6B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

data.narrative:
Black Forest Labs, an AI & Software company at Series B stage, presents a valuation case anchored to a $3.25B last post-money valuation. The peer universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir), with assessment focused on 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers including Figure AI ($39.0B Series C), Reflection AI ($25.0B Series A), and Applied Intuition ($15.0B Series A), exhibiting a wide valuation range of $5.6B to $39.0B.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the current $3.2B post-money valuation, yielding $6.2B as an implied Series C or later value. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company comparable set produces a $14.5B implied valuation, reflecting the elevated multiples currently observed across frontier AI companies at similar or adjacent stages.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $5.2B to $16.7B, with a median implied value of $10.3B. The dispersion reflects material uncertainty: the peer set spans early-stage ($8.3B–$25.0B Series A) to Series C ($14.0B–$39.0B), and Black Forest Labs' specific commercial traction, capital efficiency, and technical differentiation relative to this peer cohort remain key value drivers. The current $3.25B valuation sits below the stage-progression floor, suggesting either a discount relative to comparable stage-advancement multiples or positioning ahead of a material step-up event.
